Data.Description: Manage UiPath processes (runnable bindings of packages to folders). Processes are what you execute with 'jobs start'. Use 'packages' to manage the uploaded automation code.

Subcommands.Name: list [options]
Subcommands.Description: List processes in a folder, or use --all-folders for a tenant-wide audit. Returns process key (GUID), name, version, and type. Use the process key with 'jobs start' to execute a process.

Subcommands.Name: get [options] <process-key>
Subcommands.Description: Get detailed process information by key (GUID). Returns version, entry point, input/output argument schemas, process type, and configuration. No folder context needed — the key is globally unique. Use 'processes list' to find process keys.

Subcommands.Name: resources [options] <process-key>
Subcommands.Description: List package resources required by a process. Resolves the process folder automatically and returns referenced queues, assets, buckets, connections, and other package requirements.

Subcommands.Name: version-history [options] <process-key>
Subcommands.Description: List chronological package version history for a process. Folder is optional; if not provided, it is inferred from the process key. Returns versions previously associated with the process.

Subcommands.Name: create [options]
Subcommands.Description: Create a new process by binding a package to a folder. Requires --folder-path or --folder-key. Use the package Id from 'packages list' or 'packages get', and 'packages versions' to find available versions. After creation, start the process with 'jobs start <process-key>'.

Subcommands.Name: update-version [options] <process-keys...>
Subcommands.Description: Update one or more processes to the latest package version, or a specific version with --version. Folder is optional — if not provided, it is inferred from the process key. Running jobs are not affected — only new jobs will use the updated version. Accepts one or more process keys. Multiple keys use the bulk API. Use --version only with a single key.

Subcommands.Name: edit [options] <process-key>
Subcommands.Description: Edit process settings by key (GUID). No folder needed — resolves cross-folder. Only provided fields are updated (PATCH). Use 'processes get' to see current values.

Subcommands.Name: delete [options] <process-key>
Subcommands.Description: Delete a process binding by key (GUID). This removes the release binding from its folder and does not delete the package. Use 'processes list' to find process keys.

Subcommands.Name: rollback [options] <process-key>
Subcommands.Description: Rollback a process to its previous package version. Folder is optional — if not provided, it is inferred from the process key. Useful when a new version causes issues and you need to quickly revert.
